Video.summary: Watch Martha make chicken soup, minestrone, and spinach cream soup.
Video.title: Soups

Video.summary: Martha shares her love of houseplants. Learn all about the most popular and sought after houseplants, topiaries and begonias and how to take care of them.
Video.title: Amazing Houseplants

Video.summary: Succulents are known for their interesting textures. Martha explores how best to care for succulents including watering, fertilizing, and repotting.
Video.title: The World of Succulents

Video.summary: Martha visits the annual Orchid Show at the New York Botanical Garden, with a mesmerizing array of orchids designed by acclaimed artist Lily Kwong.
Video.title: The Incredible World of Orchids

Video.summary: With over 200 chickens and 20 different breeds, Martha shares her tips for raising and caring for her flock of chickens. Then, learn the how-to of building a chicken coop.
Video.title: Hens and Chicks

Video.summary: One of the joys of gardening is having beautiful color all year. Bulbs and tubers are at the center of the show because of their beautiful blooms from spring to winter.
Video.title: Color For All Seasons

Video.summary: Martha road trips to Arethusa Farm in Bantam, CT, to learn how they make their award-winning cheese.
Video.title: Arethusa Farm Dairy's Award Winning Cheese

Image.alt: Arethusa Farm Dairy's Award Winning Cheese
Arethusa Farm Dairy's Award Winning Cheese
Arethusa Farm Dairy's Award Winning Cheese

Video.summary: Martha gets a first-hand look behind the delicious French dishes at Cenadou Bistrot.
Video.title: French Bistro Night

Video.summary: Martha demonstrates egg recipes; Martha visits her chicken coop.
Video.title: How to Properly Cook an Egg

Image.alt: How to Properly Cook an Egg
How to Properly Cook an Egg
How to Properly Cook an Egg

Video.summary: Martha prepares onion dishes with different cooking techniques: French onion soup, balsamic-glazed pearl onions, and fried onion rings. Plus, pickled Vidalia and red onions
Video.title: Onions

Video.summary: Martha makes her favorite jam recipes.
Video.title: The Perfect Jams to Pair With a Simple Breakfast

Image.alt: The Perfect Jams to Pair With a Simple Breakfast
The Perfect Jams to Pair With a Simple Breakfast
The Perfect Jams to Pair With a Simple Breakfast

Video.summary: Martha builds a brand new, half-acre raised bed vegetable garden on her farm! It's a step-by-step guide to designing, building, preparing the soil, and planting.
Video.title: Martha Builds a Raised Bed Vegetable Garden

Image.alt: Martha Builds a Raised Bed Vegetable Garden
Martha Builds a Raised Bed Vegetable Garden
Martha Builds a Raised Bed Vegetable Garden

Video.summary: Everything is coming up roses on Martha's Bedford farm; join Martha while she plants a new garden with almost 200 roses.
Video.title: Everything's Coming Up Roses

Video.summary: Martha's woodland gardens, including her azalea grove, burst with the last flowers of spring. Everything you need to know about hostas. Plus, bird watching.
Video.title: Azaleas, Hostas, and Wild Birds

Video.summary: Peek inside Martha's gardens past and present! Martha visits her former home, Turkey Hill; then back to her home in Bedford for a garden club tour.
Video.title: Martha's Garden Tours of Turkey Hill and Bedford

Image.alt: Martha's Garden Tours of Turkey Hill and Bedford
Martha's Garden Tours of Turkey Hill and Bedford
Martha's Garden Tours of Turkey Hill and Bedford

Video.summary: Martha shares techniques for roasts: prime rib, crown roast of pork, and stuffed turkey breast.
Video.title: Perfect Roast

Video.summary: Martha blanches, steams, and roasts vegetables to enhance flavor and retain nutritional properties.
Video.title: Vegetables

Video.summary: Roasting is a technique used for both formal dinners and casual gatherings. Martha makes roast rack of lamb, salt-roasted sea bass, and saffron-roasted chicken wings
Video.title: Roasting

Video.summary: Martha shows how to make milk dough which creates soft, springy-textured bread: Japanese-style white bread, cinnamon-raisin swirl bread, and jalapeno-cheddar rolls
Video.title: White Bread

Video.summary: Martha shows us her favorite South Atlantic desserts: Virginia peanut pie, light and fluffy Lady Baltimore cake, and the ever-popular strawberry shortcake
Video.title: South Atlantic

Video.summary: Martha teaches us grain substitutions for three classics: rhubarb and raspberry rye crisp, buttermilk barley biscuits, and spelt layer cake with whipped cream and berries
Video.title: Updated Classics

Video.summary: Martha shows how to make three coffee shop favorites at home: scrumptious apple cider doughnut cake, pumpkin spelt scones with maple glaze, and seeded graham biscotti
Video.title: Coffee Shop Favorites

Video.summary: Martha creates three baked goods using unique flours beyond the white variety: herb quiche with rye crust, muesli coffee cake, and seeded savory quick bread
Video.title: Flour Power

Video.summary: Martha shows the versatility of seeds by using them in three recipes: seeded English muffins, seeded Saratoga torte, and light and crispy whole-grain seeded wafer crackers
Video.title: Seeded

Video.summary: Martha shows how to properly plant trees including measuring, fertilizing, and watering; how to start trees from seedlings. Plus, Martha's new maze.
Video.title: Martha's Amazing Maze; How to Grow Trees

Image.alt: Martha's Amazing Maze; How to Grow Trees
Martha's Amazing Maze; How to Grow Trees
Martha's Amazing Maze; How to Grow Trees

Video.summary: Martha spotlights conifers, the cone-bearing evergreen trees. Take a tour of Martha's Christmas tree farm; learn how to properly move an established tree.
Video.title: Everything You Need To Know About Conifers

Image.alt: Everything You Need To Know About Conifers
Everything You Need To Know About Conifers
Everything You Need To Know About Conifers

Video.summary: Martha's vegetable greenhouse allows her to harvest a year-round bounty. Then, how to collect, store, and sow seeds. Plus, Martha makes her green drink.
Video.title: Inside Martha's Vegetable Greenhouse

Video.summary: Martha shows how to make four vegetable dishes: steamed artichokes with tarragon butter, braised leeks, fried okra, and whole roasted garlic
Video.title: Know Your Vegetables

Video.summary: A great sauce can take a dish from good to fantastic. Martha shares recipes for bearnaise sauce, Kansas City barbecue sauce, tartar sauce, cocktail sauce, and salsa verde
Video.title: Sauces

Video.summary: There is no end to what you can make with pork. Martha makes porchetta, a holiday table-worthy glazed ham, and pork tenderloin and plums
Video.title: Pork

Video.summary: Martha offers a potato primer: scalloped potatoes, all-American potato salad, silky potato puree. Plus, crispy-on-the-outside, creamy-on-the-inside smashed potatoes
Video.title: Potatoes

Video.summary: Salads are a terrific way to make vegetables your main course. Martha makes frisee aux lardons, chopped salad, Caesar salad, and stacked butter lettuce with citrus vinaigrette
Video.title: Salads

Video.summary: Enjoy corn in many different forms! Martha demonstrates sweet and savory corn fritters, corn stock, summer corn chowder, and homemade creamed corn
Video.title: Corn

Video.summary: Martha heads to her local seafood market, Mt. Kisco Seafood, to pick-up the perfect ingredients for a clam bake; broiled salmon and delicious creamed spinach.
Video.title: Martha Throws a Clam Bake

Video.summary: Martha visits Il Buco Alimentari with chef Justin Smillie and fries artichokes to crispy perfection; they construct a salad with anchovy crumbs followed by spaghetti bottarga and charred duck.
Video.title: Rustic Italian Cuisine

Video.summary: Martha dines at the Korean restaurant NARO and begins her culinary journey with two Korean cocktails; Martha and executive chef Nate Kuester make reimagined versions of traditional Korean cuisine.
Video.title: Exploring Korean Cuisine

Video.summary: Martha prepares a brunch menu with whitefish salad from Acme Smoked Fish Corp and crafts a cocktail featuring two brunch staples; Martha makes her favorite frittata dish and bakes a rhubarb crisp.
Video.title: Martha's Favorite Brunch

Video.summary: Martha discusses how to choose and maintain the best knives; guest Chef Masa Takayama.
Video.title: Japanese Knife Skills

Video.summary: Florida Character: Palms and Banyan Trees at Fairchild Tropical Garden, Candied Citrus Peel, Orchids at Kerry's Bromeliads Nursery, Joe's Stone Crab Restaurant, Key Lime Pie
Video.title: Florida: Palm and Banyan Trees, Key Lime Pie

Image.alt: Florida: Palm and Banyan Trees, Key Lime Pie
Florida: Palm and Banyan Trees, Key Lime Pie
Florida: Palm and Banyan Trees, Key Lime Pie

Video.summary: Storing Lemon Curd Good Thing, Cake of the Month: Lemon Meringue, Arranging Tulips, Collecting Silver Tea Sets, Tip of the Day: Tea Cozy, Memory Wreath
Video.title: Lemon Meringue Cake, Arranging Tulips

Video.summary: Roller Shade Good Thing, Parmesan Pudding with Pea Sauce, Leg of Lamb, Brick House Vineyard (Field Trip), Bath Mitt Good Thing, Cookie of the Week: Kolache, Hand Tracing Good Thing
Video.title: Leg of Lamb, Bath Mitt, Kolache

Image.alt: Leg of Lamb, Bath Mitt, Kolache
Leg of Lamb, Bath Mitt, Kolache
Leg of Lamb, Bath Mitt, Kolache
